question: Under the Motor Vehicles (Third-Party Risks and Compensation) Act 1960, what type of insurance is compulsory for every motor car user in Singapore?
explanation: The Act requires every motor car user to have insurance covering legal liabilities for death or bodily injuries to third parties. This minimum is the Act Liability Only policy.
jpComparison: Japan: CALI (jibaiseki) similarly covers bodily injury to third parties only.

question: Which of the following is NOT one of the four main types of Private Motor Car Insurance in Singapore?
explanation: The four types are: Act Liability Only, Third-Party Only, Third-Party Fire & Theft, and Comprehensive.
jpComparison: Japan: Motor insurance is structured as compulsory (jibaiseki) plus voluntary (nin-i hoken).

question: What does an Act Liability Only policy cover?
choices: Death or bodily injury to third parties including passengers
Death or bodily injury to third parties and property damage
Damage to the insured motor car only
Bodily injury to the driver and passengers
explanation: Act Liability Only covers only liability for death or bodily injuries to third parties (including passengers).
jpComparison: Japan: CALI also covers bodily injury only, not property damage.

question: What additional perils does Third-Party, Fire & Theft cover compared to Third-Party Only?
choices: Accidental damage to the insured car
Fire and theft damage to the insured car
explanation: Third-Party, Fire & Theft extends Third-Party Only by protecting the insured car against fire or theft.
jpComparison: Japan: Japanese voluntary motor insurance can include fire and theft as selected perils.

question: For which type of car is Third-Party, Fire & Theft usually purchased?
choices: Brand new cars under hire purchase
Older cars, e.g. above 10 years of age
explanation: Usually purchased for older cars (above 10 years), though premium may not be significantly less.
jpComparison: Japan: Owners of older vehicles similarly choose limited coverage to save on premiums.

question: Which type of Private Motor Car Insurance provides the broadest protection?
explanation: Comprehensive offers the broadest protection including own damage, liability, medical, and PA benefits.
jpComparison: Japan: Full-coverage voluntary insurance with vehicle coverage is the closest equivalent.

question: When is Comprehensive motor insurance a requirement in Singapore?
choices: When the car purchase is financed by a loan
explanation: Comprehensive cover is required when a car is financed by a loan (hire purchase).
jpComparison: Japan: Auto loans similarly require comprehensive coverage.

question: Which motor insurance document must be shown to the police as evidence of insurance?
explanation: The Certificate of Insurance must be shown to police as and when required.
jpComparison: Japan: The CALI certificate must similarly be carried in the vehicle.

question: Which is NOT covered under Section I (Own Damage) of a Comprehensive motor policy?
explanation: Section I excludes depreciation/wear and tear, consequential loss, mechanical/electrical breakdown, and tyre-only damage.
jpComparison: Japan: Vehicle insurance similarly excludes depreciation and wear and tear.

question: Under Section II, is the indemnity limit for death or bodily injury to third parties capped?
choices: Yes, at S$1 million per person
explanation: As required under the Act, indemnity for death or bodily injury to third parties is unlimited.
jpComparison: Japan: CALI has capped limits while voluntary cover is unlimited.

question: Under Section IV, what is the death benefit payable to the insured?
explanation: Section IV death benefit for the insured is S$20,000.
jpComparison: Japan: CALI pays up to 30 million yen for death.

question: What is the NCD entitlement after one claim-free year?
explanation: NCD scale: Year 1=10%, Year 2=20%, Year 3=30%, Year 4=40%, Year 5+=50%.
jpComparison: Japan: NCD uses a 1-20 grade scale rather than percentage per year.

question: What is the maximum NCD percentage achievable?
explanation: Maximum NCD is 50% after 5+ consecutive claim-free years.
jpComparison: Japan: Maximum discount is 63% at Grade 20.

question: What does the Motor Insurers Bureau (MIB) cover?
choices: Hit-and-run and claims against uninsured drivers
Only property damage from uninsured drivers
explanation: MIB compensates victims of accidents caused by uninsured or untraced motorists.
jpComparison: Japan: Government guarantee scheme serves a similar function.

question: What does BOLA stand for?
explanation: BOLA = Barometer Of Liability Agreement, replaced KFKA on 13 May 2003.
jpComparison: Japan: Similar fault determination guidelines exist.

question: The MCF requires motor accidents to be reported within what timeframe?
explanation: Under the MCF, accidents must be reported within 24 hours.
jpComparison: Japan: No strict 24-hour deadline but prompt notification required.

question: An excess (deductible) means the insured bears which portion of a claim?
choices: Any amount above the policy limit
explanation: Excess is the first part of any claim the insurer will not pay.

question: Which agreement replaced KFKA on 13 May 2003?
explanation: BOLA replaced the Knock-for-Knock Agreement on 13 May 2003.
jpComparison: Japan: Similar knock-for-knock arrangements have evolved.

question: Under Section 2 of the Motor Vehicles Act 1960, road is defined as:
choices: Any public road and any other road to which the public has access, including bridges
explanation: Road means any public road and any other road to which the public has access, including bridges.
jpComparison: Japan: The Road Traffic Act also defines roads broadly.

question: Why is Act Liability Only generally not sold as separate cover?
choices: Most accidents involve vehicle damage so motorists opt for broader coverage
explanation: Most accidents involve vehicle damage, so motorists choose broader coverage.
jpComparison: Japan: Most drivers also purchase voluntary insurance beyond CALI.

question: Which is correct about Third-Party Only motor insurance?
choices: Covers death/bodily injury to third parties and third-party property damage
Only covers death or bodily injury
Provides medical expenses for the insured
explanation: Covers death/bodily injury AND third-party property damage, not the insured car.
jpComparison: Japan: Voluntary third-party liability similarly covers both.

question: What must the insured do if the Certificate of Insurance is lost?
choices: File a police report in 24 hours
explanation: A statutory declaration must be made.

question: Legal status of a cover note vs Certificate of Insurance?
explanation: A cover note has the same legal force.
jpComparison: Japan: Temporary certificates similarly serve as valid proof.

question: The policy document in motor insurance is best described as:
choices: Evidence of the insurance contract with terms and conditions
explanation: Evidence of the contract, not the contract itself.
jpComparison: Japan: Insurance policy similarly serves as evidence.

question: Endorsements can include which excluded perils?
choices: Flood, strike, riot, and civil commotion
explanation: Can add flood, strike, riot, and civil commotion.
jpComparison: Japan: Endorsements similarly modify standard terms.

question: Under Section I, max authorised repair cost without insurer approval?
explanation: S$300 after deduction of Own Damage Excess.
jpComparison: Japan: Typically requires insurer approval for all repairs.

question: Typical third-party property damage limit under Section II?
explanation: S$5 million per claim or series of claims from one event.
jpComparison: Japan: Commonly offers unlimited property damage cover.

question: An authorised driver under Section II is:
choices: Someone with owner permission but not named in Schedule
explanation: Has permission to drive but not named in the policy Schedule.
jpComparison: Japan: Distinguishes named and other drivers with different premiums.

question: Named drivers allowed without extra premium?
choices: Up to two qualified family members or partners
explanation: Up to two named drivers who are qualified family members or partners.
jpComparison: Japan: Allowances vary by insurer and driver age.

question: Typical medical expense limit per person per accident under Section III?
explanation: S$300 to S$1,000 per person per accident.
jpComparison: Japan: Covers actual expenses up to policy limits.

question: Section IV benefit for total loss of sight in one eye (insured)?
explanation: One eye = S$10,000; both eyes = S$20,000.
jpComparison: Japan: PA benefits use disability grades rather than fixed amounts.

question: Under Section IV, passengers entitled to what proportion vs insured?
explanation: Passengers get half the Scale of Compensation amounts.
jpComparison: Japan: PA riders typically cover all occupants equally.

question: Which is an exclusion under Section IV?
choices: Self-inflicted injury, suicide, or attempted suicide
explanation: Excludes self-inflicted injury, suicide, intoxication, pregnancy/childbirth.
jpComparison: Japan: PA insurance also excludes self-inflicted injuries.

question: Multiple policies same insurer - Section IV benefits from:
explanation: Only entitled to payment from one policy.

question: If occupants exceed legal seating capacity, Section IV benefits?
choices: No benefits or rateable proportion only
explanation: Some insurers pay nothing; others pay rateable proportion only.
jpComparison: Japan: PA riders may reduce benefits if exceeded.

question: NCD after 3 consecutive claim-free years?
jpComparison: Japan: Grade system improves 1 grade per claim-free year.

question: 50% NCD with one claim - NCD at renewal?
explanation: 50% with one claim drops to 20%.
jpComparison: Japan: One claim causes a 3-grade drop.

question: 30% NCD with one claim - NCD at renewal?
explanation: 30% with one claim drops to Nil (0%).
jpComparison: Japan: Lower levels face steeper drops.

question: What is the Certificate of Merit (COM) discount?
explanation: 5% discount for 3 consecutive demerit-free years.
jpComparison: Japan: Gold Licence discount similarly rewards safe driving.

question: Geographical coverage of a Singapore motor policy?
choices: Singapore, West Malaysia, and within 80.5 km of Thailand border
explanation: Singapore, West Malaysia, and within 80.5 km of the Thailand-Malaysia border.
jpComparison: Japan: Motor insurance covers Japan only.

question: Upon receiving a writ or summons, the insured must:
choices: Forward it to the insurer immediately
explanation: Must forward any legal process to the insurer immediately.

question: Elderly, Young, Inexperienced Drivers excess applies to:
choices: Under 25, over 65, or less than 2 years experience
explanation: Additional excess for young (under 25), elderly (over 65), or inexperienced (under 2 years).
jpComparison: Japan: Age-based rating serves similar purposes.

question: How can an insurer cancel a motor policy?
explanation: Written notice, typically 7 days.

question: Which is a general exclusion for the entire motor policy?
explanation: General exclusions include nuclear risks, war, and terrorism.
jpComparison: Japan: Nuclear and war exclusions are standard globally.

question: The windscreen extension covers:
explanation: Covers the windscreen and all windows.
jpComparison: Japan: Part of standard vehicle damage cover.

question: What is the NCD Protector extension?
choices: Allows one at-fault claim per year without affecting NCD
explanation: Allows one at-fault claim per year without NCD impact.
jpComparison: Japan: Some insurers offer similar riders.

question: When was the MCF introduced?
explanation: MCF introduced 1 June 2008.
jpComparison: Japan: Motor claims system evolved gradually.

question: When was the MIB established?
explanation: MIB established 22 February 1975.

question: What is the doctrine of volenti in motor insurance?
choices: A person knowingly entering danger cannot claim for resulting injuries
explanation: A person who knowingly enters a dangerous situation cannot claim for resulting injuries.

question: Legal defence cost under Section II does NOT cover:
choices: Charge of causing death by dangerous driving
A murder charge from car use
explanation: Legal defence (up to S$3,000) excludes murder charges.
jpComparison: Japan: Legal expense riders also exclude intentional acts.

question: 40% NCD with two claims - NCD at renewal?
explanation: 40% with two or more claims drops to Nil.
jpComparison: Japan: Two claims cause a 6-grade drop.

question: FIDReC adjudication limit as of 1 July 2024?
jpComparison: Japan: Financial ADR has no fixed cap.

question: When was FIDReC established?
explanation: Established August 2005.

question: Usage Based Insurance (UBI) relies on:
choices: Telematics devices or apps monitoring driving behaviour
explanation: Telematics monitors speed, braking, acceleration, cornering, and driving time.
jpComparison: Japan: Telematics insurance growing with app-based scores.

question: What considerations apply to insuring electric vehicles?
choices: Battery cost, charging risks, and higher repair costs
explanation: Battery replacement cost, charging risks, and specialised repair costs.
jpComparison: Japan: EV-specific riders cover battery and charging equipment.

question: What is the GIFT scheme for?
choices: Standardise towing and storage charges after accidents
explanation: Standardises towing and storage charges.
jpComparison: Japan: JAF provides standardised assistance; towing regulated separately.

question: Under Non-Injury Motor Accident Reporting Scheme:
choices: All accidents go to police first
Non-injury accidents go directly to insurers via approved centres
explanation: Non-injury accidents go directly to insurers through approved centres.
jpComparison: Japan: All accidents require police reporting regardless of injury.

question: Motorcycle third-party property damage limit?
explanation: S$500,000 for motorcycles vs S$5 million for cars.
jpComparison: Japan: Motorcycle and car insurance share the same limits.

question: Motorcycle overseas theft benefit?
explanation: 50% of loss up to S$2,500.
jpComparison: Japan: Motorcycle insurance rarely covers overseas theft.

question: What is Maco used for?
choices: Electronic filing and tracking of motor accident claims
explanation: Electronic filing and tracking of motor accident claims.
jpComparison: Japan: Insurers use proprietary portals rather than industry-wide system.

question: What is the primary purpose of Personal Accident (PA) Insurance?
choices: A) To provide savings and investment benefits
B) To provide financial protection against accidental death or bodily injury
C) To cover death from natural causes such as disease
D) To replace life insurance policies
explanation: PA Insurance provides financial protection and financial burden relief to the insured against the risk of accidental death or bodily injury. It pays a monetary sum to beneficiaries upon accidental death and helps mitigate potential loss of income.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険も同様に、急激・偶然・外来の事故による身体の傷害を補償する目的で設計されています。基本的な目的はシンガポールと共通です。

question: A PA Insurance policy is classified as which type of contract?
explanation: A PA Insurance policy is generally a benefit policy — a contract to pay a sum of money in the event of a specified contingency, irrespective of whether or not the insured sustains a financial loss.
jpComparison: 日本の傷害保険も定額払い（benefit policy）の性質を持ちます。実際の損害額に関係なく、契約時に定めた保険金額が支払われる点は同じです。

question: Under a per trip Travel Insurance policy, when does coverage typically end after the insured returns to Singapore?
choices: A) Immediately upon landing in Singapore
B) Up to 3 hours after return to Singapore
C) 24 hours after return to Singapore
D) At midnight on the day of return
explanation: Per trip Travel Insurance coverage ends when the insured arrives back at his residence, usually up to 3 hours after the return to Singapore.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険も自宅到着までが補償期間だが、シンガポールでは帰国後3時間以内という明確な時間制限がある点が特徴的。

question: What is the maximum length of a single trip typically allowed under a per trip Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: The maximum length of a single trip allowed under a per trip policy ranges from 180 to 185 days.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険は通常最長2年まで対応可能だが、シンガポールの1回渡航型は180～185日が上限。

question: Under an annual Travel Insurance policy, what is the typical maximum duration allowed for each individual trip?
explanation: Under an annual Travel Insurance policy, each individual trip is typically limited to 90 to 92 consecutive days.
jpComparison: 日本の年間海外旅行保険でも1回の渡航期間に制限があるが、シンガポールでは90～92日が標準。

question: Under a Family Plan Travel Insurance policy, which of the following statements about children is CORRECT?
choices: A) Children can travel independently without an adult policyholder
B) Children must be accompanied by an insured adult on the same trip
C) Only children under age 12 are eligible for coverage
D) A maximum of two children can be covered per family
explanation: Under a Family Plan, children must be accompanied by an insured adult on the same trip.
jpComparison: 日本の家族型海外旅行保険も家族を一括でカバーするが、子供が成人と同行する必要があるかは保険会社により異なる。シンガポールでは同行が条件。

question: What is the typical age limit for children to be covered under a Family Plan Travel Insurance policy?
choices: B) Under 18 or 21 years old, extendable to 25 if a full-time student
D) Under 30 years old if financially dependent
explanation: Children are typically eligible up to age 18 or 21, extendable to 25 if a full-time student.
jpComparison: 日本の家族特約では年齢制限が比較的緩やかだが、シンガポールでは18/21歳を基準とし、フルタイム学生なら25歳まで延長可能。

question: Which of the following is NOT a typical premium classification zone for Travel Insurance in Singapore?
explanation: Travel Insurance premiums are typically classified by three zones: ASEAN, Asia, and Worldwide. No separate European Union zone.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険は地域別で細かく保険料が分かれるが、シンガポールではASEAN・アジア・全世界の3区分。

question: At what age do Travel Insurance policyholders in Singapore typically face restricted coverage limits?
explanation: Coverage limits are typically restricted for policyholders aged 70 and above.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険も高齢者は制限があるが、シンガポールでは70歳が補償制限の基準年齢。

question: What is the Personal Accident capital sum insured under a typical Deluxe Plan Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: Under a typical Deluxe Plan, the PA capital sum insured is S$500,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の死亡・後遺障害補償は1,000万～5,000万円が一般的。シンガポールのデラックスプランS$500,000は日本の上位プランと同等水準。

question: What does the Double Indemnity benefit in a Travel Insurance policy provide?
choices: A) Double the medical expense coverage for overseas treatment
B) Double the PA capital sum if death/injury occurs while travelling as a fare-paying passenger on a common carrier
C) Double the baggage loss compensation for international flights
D) Double the travel delay benefit for delays exceeding 12 hours
explanation: Double Indemnity provides double the PA capital sum if death or permanent disability occurs while travelling as a fare-paying passenger on a common carrier.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険には公共交通乗用具搭乗中の倍額補償はあまり見られないが、国内傷害保険では交通事故傷害特約として類似の仕組みがある。

question: What is the typical maximum limit for Medical Expenses coverage under a comprehensive Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: A comprehensive Travel Insurance policy typically provides medical expense coverage up to S$2,000,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の治療費用は無制限プランが主流。シンガポールではS$2,000,000が上限。

question: Within how many days of returning to Singapore must follow-up medical treatment commence to be covered?
explanation: Follow-up medical treatment in Singapore must commence within 31 days of return.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険では帰国後の治療は通常30日以内の継続治療が条件。シンガポールの31日以内はほぼ同等。

question: What is the typical sub-limit for Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) treatment under a Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: TCM treatment is typically covered up to a sub-limit of S$750.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険では漢方・東洋医学の補償は一般的でないが、シンガポールではTCMが広く利用されS$750の限度額が設定。

question: Under which condition is a Hospital Visit benefit payable under a Travel Insurance policy?
choices: A) The insured is hospitalised overseas for more than 3 days
B) The insured is hospitalised overseas for more than 5 days
C) The insured is hospitalised in Singapore for more than 7 days
D) Any hospital admission regardless of duration
explanation: The Hospital Visit benefit is payable when the insured is hospitalised overseas for more than 5 consecutive days.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険にも救援者費用特約があり3日以上の入院で家族の渡航費用が補償される。シンガポールでは5日超が条件。

question: Regarding Hospital Visit and Compassionate Visit benefits, which statement is CORRECT?
choices: A) Both benefits can be claimed simultaneously for the same event
B) Only one of the two benefits can be claimed per event - they are mutually exclusive
C) Hospital Visit is for domestic hospitalisation and Compassionate Visit is for overseas
D) Compassionate Visit covers the insured own travel, not a family member
explanation: Hospital Visit and Compassionate Visit benefits are mutually exclusive.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の救援者費用は入院見舞いと死亡時の渡航費用を包括的にカバーするが、シンガポールでは別建てかつ排他的。

question: What is the daily Hospital Confinement benefit for overseas hospitalisation under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: The Hospital Confinement benefit typically pays S$200 per day overseas and S$100 per day for follow-up in Singapore.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険には入院日額の概念はなく治療費実費を補償。シンガポールでは日額S$200の入院給付金が損害保険に組み込まれている。

question: What happens to the Hospital Confinement benefit when the insured is admitted to an ICU?
choices: A) The benefit remains the same as standard ward rate
D) ICU stays are not covered under Hospital Confinement
explanation: When the insured is admitted to an ICU, the Hospital Confinement benefit is typically doubled.
jpComparison: 日本の入院給付金でもICU加算がある商品は存在するが、シンガポールの旅行保険でICU時の倍額支給が標準的に組み込まれている。

question: What is the typical Emergency Medical Evacuation limit under a higher-tier Travel Insurance plan?
explanation: Under a higher-tier plan, Emergency Medical Evacuation coverage is typically unlimited.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険でも救援者費用は高額プランで無制限が一般的。シンガポールも上位プランでは無制限。

question: What is the maximum total benefit for Kidnap and Hostage coverage under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
choices: A) S$250 per day, maximum S$5,000
B) S$250 per day, maximum S$10,000
C) S$500 per day, maximum S$10,000
D) S$500 per day, maximum S$20,000
explanation: Kidnap and Hostage coverage typically pays S$250 per day, maximum S$10,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険には誘拐・人質補償は通常含まれない。シンガポールがこれを標準補償に含めている点は地域のリスク認識の違いを反映。

question: What is the maximum Travel Curtailment benefit under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: Travel Curtailment benefit is typically capped at S$20,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険では旅行短縮費用は特約として付帯される。シンガポールのS$20,000上限は明確な上限設定。

question: What is the maximum Travel Cancellation benefit under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: Travel Cancellation benefit is typically capped at S$15,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険のキャンセル費用特約は旅行代金の全額までカバーするものが多い。シンガポールはS$15,000の定額上限。

question: After how many hours of travel delay does the Travel Delay benefit become payable?
explanation: The Travel Delay benefit becomes payable after 6 consecutive hours, paying S$200 per 6 hours, max S$2,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の航空機遅延費用は6時間超の遅延で実費補償。シンガポールでは6時間ごとにS$200の定額支給。

question: Under a Travel Insurance policy, how is haze classified as a cause of travel delay?
explanation: Haze is classified as a man-made event, as it results from human activities such as agricultural burning.
jpComparison: 日本ではヘイズによる航空便遅延は一般的でないが、東南アジアでは焼畑農業等による越境ヘイズが深刻な問題。人為的事象としての分類は保険適用に影響する。

question: What is the benefit amount for an Overbooked Flight under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: The Overbooked Flight benefit pays a flat S$200.
jpComparison: 日本では航空会社のオーバーブッキングに対する補償は航空会社自身が行うのが一般的。シンガポールの旅行保険がS$200の定額給付を提供する点はユニーク。

question: What is the benefit amount for Travel Misconnection under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: The Travel Misconnection benefit pays S$500 if the insured misses a connecting flight due to late arrival of prior transport.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険では乗継遅延費用は実費補償が一般的。シンガポールではS$500の定額支給。

question: If the same event causes both an overbooked flight and a travel misconnection, how does the policy respond?
choices: A) Both benefits (S$200 + S$500) are payable
B) Only the higher benefit (S$500) is payable
C) Only one of the two benefits can be claimed
D) The insured may choose which to claim plus 50% of the other
explanation: If the same event gives rise to both, only one of the two benefits can be claimed.
jpComparison: 日本の保険でも同一事故に対する重複給付の制限は一般的。シンガポールではオーバーブッキングとミスコネクションの排他適用が明示されている。

question: After how many hours of delay does the Baggage Delay benefit become payable?
explanation: Baggage Delay benefit becomes payable after 6 consecutive hours, paying S$200 per 6 hours, max S$1,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の手荷物遅延費用は6時間超の遅延で実費補償。シンガポールでは6時間ごとにS$200の定額で最大S$1,000。

question: What is the typical per-item sub-limit for Personal Baggage loss or damage under a Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: Personal Baggage coverage has a per-item/pair/set sub-limit of S$500.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の携行品損害も1品あたりの上限（通常10万円）がある。シンガポールのS$500は日本より低い水準。

question: What is the Personal Liability coverage limit under a typical Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: Personal Liability coverage is typically up to S$2,000,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の個人賠償責任は1億円が標準的。シンガポールのS$2,000,000は日本の標準より高い水準。

question: The Rental Vehicle Excess benefit covers the deductible on a rental vehicle claim. From which type of agency must the vehicle be rented?
choices: A) Any vehicle rental service including peer-to-peer platforms
B) A licensed car rental agency only
C) Any agency approved by the insurer
D) Only agencies with comprehensive insurance coverage
explanation: The Rental Vehicle Excess benefit only applies when the vehicle is rented from a licensed car rental agency.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険でもレンタカーの免責額補償特約があるが、正規レンタカー会社からの借用が条件である点は同様。

question: By how many days is the policy period automatically extended if return is delayed due to transport disruption?
explanation: The policy period is automatically extended by 14 days for transport disruption.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険も帰国遅延時の自動延長があるが、シンガポールの14日間自動延長は明確な基準。

question: If the insured falls ill overseas and cannot return on time, by how many days is the policy automatically extended?
explanation: If unable to return due to illness, the policy is automatically extended by 30 days.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険では傷病による帰国遅延の自動延長は保険会社により異なる。シンガポールの30日間自動延長は大幅に長い。

question: How is GST treated for Travel Insurance premiums in Singapore?
choices: A) Standard GST rate of 9% applies
B) Reduced GST rate of 5% applies
C) Zero-rated under Section 21(3)(c) of the GST Act
D) GST-exempt as a financial service
explanation: Travel Insurance premiums are zero-rated under Section 21(3)(c) of the GST Act, as the insurance covers risks occurring outside Singapore.
jpComparison: 日本では保険料は消費税非課税。シンガポールでは海外リスクを補償する旅行保険はGSTゼロ税率という独自の扱い。非課税とゼロ税率は異なる概念。

question: Within how many days of an insured event must a Travel Insurance claim generally be submitted?
explanation: Claims must generally be submitted within 21 to 30 days of the insured event.
jpComparison: 日本の損害保険の保険金請求期限は事故発生から3年（保険法第95条）と長い。シンガポールの21～30日以内は非常に短い。

question: Under STB insolvency protection, what is the minimum package tour cost that triggers the Travel Insurance requirement?
explanation: Travel Insurance for tour operator insolvency is triggered when the package tour costs S$1,000 or more (or deposit of S$500 or more).
jpComparison: 日本では旅行業法に基づく営業保証金制度で旅行者を保護する。シンガポールではSTBが旅行代金S$1,000以上で保険加入を条件としている。

question: Which of the following countries is typically EXCLUDED from Travel Insurance coverage?
explanation: Afghanistan is typically excluded, along with Cuba, DRC, Iran, Iraq, Liberia, Somalia, Sudan, and Syria.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険も戦争・内乱地域は免責だが、国名を明示的に列挙する方式はシンガポール特有。

question: Which of the following is NOT a typical general exclusion under a Travel Insurance policy?
choices: B) Losses arising from war or civil unrest
C) Injuries sustained during amateur recreational sports
explanation: Amateur recreational sports are generally covered. Typical exclusions include pre-existing conditions, war/civil unrest, self-inflicted injuries, and professional/hazardous sports.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険もアマチュアスポーツは基本補償の範囲内。プロスポーツや危険なスポーツは除外される点は共通。

question: What is the minimum cover period for a per trip Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: Insurers usually provide a minimum of three days cover for a per trip policy.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険は1日から加入可能な商品が多い。シンガポールの最低3日間は短期出張ではやや不便。

question: Which treatment is typically NOT covered under the Medical Expenses section of a Travel Insurance policy?
choices: B) Dental treatment required due to an accident
C) Elective cosmetic surgery performed overseas
D) Follow-up treatment in Singapore within 31 days of return
explanation: Elective cosmetic surgery is excluded from Medical Expenses coverage.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険でも美容整形は免責。事故による歯科治療の補償は日本でも特約で対応する点が類似。

question: What is the PA capital sum insured under a typical Classic Plan Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: Under a Classic Plan, the PA capital sum insured is S$200,000, compared to S$500,000 under a Deluxe Plan.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険のスタンダードプランでは死亡・後遺障害1,000万～3,000万円が一般的。シンガポールのクラシックプランS$200,000は同等水準。

question: Under the Credit Card Indemnity benefit, within how many hours of discovering the loss must the insured report it?
explanation: The insured must report the loss within 6 hours of discovering it.
jpComparison: 日本のクレジットカード盗難保険では届出期限は60日以内が一般的。シンガポールの6時間以内という要件は非常に厳格。

question: What peril does the Home Contents benefit under a Travel Insurance policy cover, and what is its typical limit?
choices: A) Theft during absence, up to S$5,000
B) Fire only, up to S$5,000
C) All risks including flood and theft, up to S$10,000
D) Burglary only, up to S$3,000
explanation: The Home Contents benefit covers loss or damage caused by fire only, limit S$5,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険には留守宅の家財補償はない。シンガポールの旅行保険が火災限定でS$5,000の留守宅補償を含む点は独特。

question: An insured person suffers a travel delay of 18 hours. How much Travel Delay benefit is payable?
explanation: Travel Delay pays S$200 per 6 hours. For 18 hours: S$200 at 6h + S$200 at 12h + S$200 at 18h = S$600.
jpComparison: 日本の航空機遅延費用保険は実費補償のため、遅延時間と支払額の関係はシンガポールの定額方式より柔軟だが計算は複雑。

question: Which of the following is a valid reason for a Travel Cancellation claim?
choices: A) The insured simply changed their mind about the trip
B) The employer cancelled their leave approval
C) Serious illness or death of an immediate family member
D) The insured found a cheaper travel deal
explanation: Travel Cancellation applies to unavoidable circumstances such as serious illness, injury, or death of the insured or immediate family member.
jpComparison: 日本の旅行キャンセル保険も病気・死亡等のやむを得ない事由が条件。自己都合キャンセルが免責なのは共通。

question: What is the typical maximum Baggage Delay benefit under a Travel Insurance policy?
explanation: The maximum Baggage Delay benefit is typically S$1,000.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の手荷物遅延費用は10万円程度が上限。シンガポールのS$1,000はほぼ同水準。

question: Which statement about the Financial Collapse of Travel Agency benefit is CORRECT?
choices: A) It covers any travel agency that goes bankrupt
B) It only applies to travel agencies licensed by STB
C) It requires a package tour costing at least S$500
D) Coverage is limited to S$5,000 per claim
explanation: The Financial Collapse benefit only applies to travel agencies licensed by the Singapore Tourism Board (STB).
jpComparison: 日本の旅行業法では営業保証金制度で消費者を保護。シンガポールではSTBライセンスを持つ旅行会社に限定して保険でカバー。

question: Some insurers allow two adults (not necessarily husband and wife) under a Family Plan for a single trip. What does this flexibility accommodate?
choices: B) Modern family structures such as unmarried couples with children
C) Friends travelling as a group
D) Extended family members like grandparents
explanation: Some companies allow two adults, not necessarily husband and wife, and their children to be treated as a family, accommodating modern family structures.
jpComparison: 日本の家族型海外旅行保険は法律上の配偶者・子供が対象のものが多い。シンガポールでは事実婚にも対応する柔軟な家族定義を持つ保険会社がある。

question: What is the Emergency Medical Evacuation limit under a lower-tier Travel Insurance plan?
explanation: Under a lower-tier plan, Emergency Medical Evacuation is typically S$500,000, compared to unlimited under higher-tier plans.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険の救援者費用は300万～500万円程度が多い。シンガポールの下位プランでもS$500,000と日本より大幅に高い。

question: Which claim document is typically required for a Travel Delay claim?
choices: A) Medical certificate from an overseas hospital
B) Written confirmation from the airline/carrier confirming the delay and duration
C) Police report filed within 24 hours
D) Receipts for all purchases made during the delay
explanation: Written confirmation from the airline or carrier confirming the delay and duration is the key document.
jpComparison: 日本の航空機遅延費用保険でも航空会社の遅延証明書が必須。シンガポールは定額支給のため航空会社の証明が主要書類。

question: Which correctly describes the Loss of Use of Hotel Facilities benefit?
choices: A) Compensation for hotel room damage caused by the insured
B) Coverage for loss of prepaid hotel facilities (e.g. spa, golf) due to illness or injury during the trip
D) Coverage for theft of personal items from the hotel room
explanation: This benefit covers loss of prepaid, non-refundable hotel facility charges when the insured cannot use them due to illness or injury.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険にはホテル施設利用不能の補償は一般的でない。シンガポールの旅行保険がプリペイド施設費用をカバーする点はきめ細かい補償設計。

question: Under the Terrorism Cover section of a Travel Insurance policy, which of the following is typically covered?
choices: A) Loss of personal property due to a terrorist attack
B) Medical expenses arising from injuries sustained in a terrorist attack overseas
C) Trip cancellation due to a terrorism threat level being raised
D) Loss of income due to travel disruption caused by a terrorist incident
explanation: Terrorism Cover typically covers medical expenses and PA benefits from injuries sustained in an act of terrorism overseas.
jpComparison: 日本の海外旅行保険でもテロによる傷害は補償対象だが、明示的なテロリズム補償セクションを設けているシンガポールの方式は補償範囲が明確。

question: Liability can arise at law under which three sources?
choices: A) Statute, Common Law, and Contract
C) Criminal Law, Civil Law, and Regulation
explanation: Liability can arise under Statute (laws passed by parliament), at Common Law (unwritten law), and under Contract (agreement between parties).
jpComparison: 日本でも法的責任は制定法、判例法（民法の不法行為等）、契約の3つから生じる。シンガポールのCommon Law体系はイギリス法の影響を受けている点が異なる。

question: Which of the following best describes a tort?
choices: A) A criminal offence prosecuted by the state
B) A civil wrong that violates the rights of another
C) A breach of statutory duty imposed by parliament
D) A contractual obligation between two parties
explanation: A tort is a civil wrong that violates the rights of another person. Unlike crimes, torts are part of civil law and concern private relationships between people.
jpComparison: 日本の民法における不法行為（第709条）に相当する概念。シンガポールではCommon Lawの体系でtortとして扱われる。

question: What are the four factors that must be proved to establish negligence?
choices: A) Intent, action, damage, and causation
B) Duty of care, breach of duty, damage, and proximate cause
C) Recklessness, injury, compensation, and liability
D) Fault, loss, insurance, and restitution
explanation: To establish negligence, four factors must be proved: the defendant owed a duty of care, there was a breach of that duty, there was damage or loss, and the damage was caused by that breach (proximate cause).
jpComparison: 日本の不法行為（民法709条）の成立要件は故意・過失、権利侵害、損害、因果関係の4つ。シンガポールのnegligenceの4要件と概念的に類似している。

question: What is the neighbour test used to determine in negligence law?
choices: A) Whether the defendant intended to cause harm
B) Whether a duty of care exists
C) The amount of damages payable
D) Whether the defendant was insured
explanation: The neighbour test determines whether a duty of care exists. A person must take reasonable care to avoid acts or omissions that he can reasonably foresee will injure his neighbour.
jpComparison: 日本の不法行為法にも予見可能性の概念があるが、シンガポールではneighbour testという判例法上の基準がduty of careの存否を判断する。

question: To which type of person does a property owner owe the greatest degree of care?
explanation: A property owner owes the greatest degree of care to an invitee (a person invited onto the premises with potential benefit to the owner, e.g. a customer), less to a licensee, and least to a trespasser.
jpComparison: 日本の民法では土地工作物責任（717条）があるが、来訪者の種類による注意義務の段階的区分はシンガポールのCommon Law特有の概念。

question: What is proximate cause in the context of negligence?
choices: A) The most recent event before the loss
B) An action that in a natural and continuous sequence produces the loss
C) The first event in a chain of events
D) Any event that contributes to the loss
explanation: The proximate cause is an action that, in a natural and continuous sequence, produces the loss. The sequence must be unbroken by other factors.
jpComparison: 日本の保険法でも近因（proximate cause）の概念は存在する。シンガポールのCommon Lawでは因果の連鎖が途切れない限り最初の事象が近因となる。

question: What happens when an independent action breaks the chain of causation?
choices: A) The original cause remains the proximate cause
B) The intervening cause becomes the new proximate cause
C) Both causes are considered equally responsible
D) The insurer must pay for both causes
explanation: When an independent action breaks the chain of causation and sets in motion a new chain of events, this intervening cause becomes the proximate cause.
jpComparison: 日本の保険法でも介在原因（intervening cause）により因果関係が断たれることがある。シンガポールでは介在原因が新たな近因となるとされる。

question: What is absolute (strict) liability?
choices: A) Liability that requires proof of negligence
B) Liability imposed by law on those in especially hazardous activities, even without negligence
C) Liability only for intentional acts
D) Liability that applies only to professionals
explanation: Absolute liability is imposed by law on those participating in activities judged especially hazardous. Individuals may be held liable even without negligence.
jpComparison: 日本でも無過失責任の概念があり、原子力損害賠償法や製造物責任法がこれに該当。シンガポールでは危険物・危険作業・危険動物に関して絶対責任が課される。

question: Absolute liability is most frequently applied to activities involving which of the following?
choices: A) Professional services, financial advice, and medical practice
B) Dangerous materials, hazardous operations, and dangerous animals
C) Motor vehicles, watercraft, and aircraft
explanation: Absolute liability is most frequently applied to activities involving dangerous materials, hazardous operations, or dangerous animals.
jpComparison: 日本の危険責任（報償責任）も危険な物質や動物に関連するが、シンガポールでは3カテゴリーが明示的に定義されている。

question: What is vicarious (imputed) liability?
choices: A) Liability for damage to property under your care
B) Liability transferred from one person to another who is in control
C) Liability arising from breach of contract
D) Liability that is shared equally between two parties
explanation: Vicarious liability occurs when a person liable for an occurrence is under the control of another, transferring liability to the party in control. For example, an employer may be vicariously liable for employee actions.
jpComparison: 日本の民法715条（使用者責任）に相当する概念。シンガポールでもvicarious liabilityにより使用者が被用者の不法行為について責任を負う。

question: In the context of liability insurance, who is referred to as the third party?
choices: C) The person who has suffered injury or damage and is not a named party to the insurance contract
D) The insurance broker who arranged the policy
explanation: The insured is the first party, the insurance company is the second party, and the third party is the person who suffered injury or damage and is not named in the insurance contract.
jpComparison: 日本の損害保険でも第三者（被害者）の概念は同じ。保険契約者が第一者、保険会社が第二者、被害者が第三者という構図。

question: Which type of legal liability does Personal Liability Insurance cover?
choices: A) Liability under Statute, Common Law, and Contract
B) Liability at Common Law only
explanation: Personal Liability Insurance provides coverage for legal liability at Common Law only. Commercial lines liability insurance covers Statute, Common Law, and/or Contract.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険も民法上の不法行為責任が主な補償対象。シンガポールではCommon Law上の責任のみと明確に限定されている。

question: What does Personal Liability Insurance indemnify the insured against?
choices: A) Only property damage to third parties
B) Only bodily injury to third parties
C) Accidental death, bodily injury, illness, and accidental property damage
D) Financial losses from professional negligence
explanation: Personal Liability Insurance indemnifies for claims of accidental death, bodily injury or illness to any person, and accidental loss or damage to property.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険も他人への身体傷害と財物損壊の両方を補償する点は同様。

question: What is the typical geographical limit for Personal Liability Insurance in Singapore?
choices: C) Anywhere within the Republic of Singapore
explanation: The coverage applies for occurrences during the insurance period and within the geographical limits, which is usually anywhere within the Republic of Singapore.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険は日本国内が基本だが、海外旅行保険に付帯する場合は海外も対象。シンガポールの個人賠償はシンガポール国内限定が標準。

question: Which of the following is an exclusion under Personal Liability Insurance?
choices: A) Liability for a guest slipping on a waxed floor at the insured home
B) Bodily injury to a member of the insured household
C) Liability for the insured dog biting a postman
D) Property damage caused by fire spreading from the insured home
explanation: Bodily injury to any member of the insured household is excluded, as this is not a third-party loss. The policy only covers liability to third parties.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険でも同居親族への賠償は免責。第三者への賠償のみが対象という原則は共通。

question: Liability for damage to property owned by or under the care, custody, or control of the insured is excluded under Personal Liability Insurance. Why?
choices: A) Such damage is too expensive to insure
B) It is not considered a third-party loss
C) It is already covered by fire insurance
D) The insured cannot sue themselves
explanation: Damage to the insured own property or property under their care is excluded because it is not a third-party loss. Personal Liability only covers liability to third parties.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険でも自己の財物や管理下の財物の損害は免責。賠償責任保険は第三者への賠償のみが対象。

question: Why is motor vehicle-related liability excluded from Personal Liability Insurance?
choices: A) Motor vehicles are too dangerous to insure
B) It is more appropriately covered by a Motor Insurance policy
C) Personal Liability only covers indoor incidents
D) Motor vehicle claims are always fraudulent
explanation: Motor vehicle-related liability is excluded because it is more appropriately covered by a separate Motor Insurance policy, which is compulsory in Singapore.
jpComparison: 日本でも自動車事故の賠償責任は自動車保険（自賠責＋任意保険）でカバーするため、個人賠償責任保険からは免責される。

question: What are exemplary (punitive) damages, and are they covered under Personal Liability Insurance?
choices: A) Compensatory damages for actual loss; yes, they are covered
B) Damages awarded to punish the wrongdoer; no, they are excluded
C) Damages for emotional distress; yes, they are covered
D) Court-ordered fines; yes, they are covered as legal costs
explanation: Exemplary or punitive damages are awarded to punish the wrongdoer and do not represent compensation for actual losses. They are excluded from Personal Liability Insurance.
jpComparison: 日本の民法には懲罰的損害賠償の概念がないが、シンガポールのCommon Lawでは存在し、保険で補償不可とされる。この違いは法体系の根本的差異を反映。

question: Under the Jurisdiction Clause of a Personal Liability Insurance policy, compensation for damages from courts of which country is covered?
choices: B) Singapore and neighbouring ASEAN countries
explanation: The Jurisdiction Clause limits coverage to compensation awarded by courts of competent jurisdiction within the Republic of Singapore. Judgments from other countries are excluded.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険も通常は日本国内の裁判所の判決が対象。シンガポールも同様に自国の裁判所の判決に限定している。

question: Which of the following is covered under a standard Personal Liability Insurance policy?
choices: A) Professional negligence by the insured
B) An employee injury arising in the course of employment
C) The insured dog biting and injuring a postman
D) Damage to property under the insured care and control
explanation: A dog biting a postman is a third-party liability arising from an accidental event within Singapore, which is covered. Professional negligence, employee injuries, and damage to property under care are all excluded.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険でもペットによる咬傷事故は補償対象。飼い犬の責任は民法718条（動物占有者の責任）で規定されている。

question: What is the insured NOT allowed to do without the insurer written consent when a liability claim arises?
choices: A) Report the claim to the police
B) Admit liability or make an offer or promise of payment
C) Seek medical treatment for the injured party
D) Contact a lawyer for legal advice
explanation: The insured must not admit liability or make any offer or promise of payment without the insurer written consent. This protects the insurer right to manage the claim.
jpComparison: 日本の損害保険でも被保険者が保険会社の承認なく示談や賠償を約束することは禁じられている。この条件は日本・シンガポール共通。

question: What must the insured do immediately upon receiving legal documents (writs, summons) related to a claim?
choices: A) Respond to the claimant directly
B) Forward them immediately to the insurer
C) Destroy them to avoid liability
D) File them with the police
explanation: The insured must immediately forward to the insurer all correspondence, writs, summons, or other legal documents received or served on the insured.
jpComparison: 日本の賠償責任保険でも訴訟書類の速やかな保険会社への転送は標準的な保険条件。

question: Under which statute is Work Injury Compensation Insurance compulsory in Singapore?
choices: D) Workplace Safety and Health Act 2006
explanation: Work Injury Compensation Insurance is compulsory under Section 24(1) of the Work Injury Compensation Act 2019.
jpComparison: 日本の労災保険（労働者災害補償保険）に相当する制度。シンガポールではWork Injury Compensation Act 2019が根拠法。

question: Which section of the Insurance Act 1966 requires insurance broking companies to maintain Professional Indemnity Insurance?
explanation: Section 77(1)(c) of the Insurance Act 1966 requires insurance broking companies to have in force a Professional Indemnity Insurance policy.
jpComparison: 日本でも保険仲立人（ブローカー）は保証金供託またはPI保険の付保が義務。シンガポールではInsurance Act Section 77(1)(c)で明確に規定。

question: When was Personal Mobility Insurance first launched in Singapore?
explanation: Personal Mobility Insurance was first launched in 2016, targeting users of active mobility devices.
jpComparison: 日本では電動キックボード等の保険は2023年の法改正以降に普及し始めた。シンガポールは2016年と比較的早い段階でPMD保険を導入。

question: What is the typical annual premium range for Personal Mobility Insurance in Singapore?
explanation: Personal Mobility Insurance yearly premiums range from S$65 to S$95.
jpComparison: 日本の電動キックボード保険は自動車保険の特約で対応する場合が多く、単独商品の保険料設定はシンガポールとは異なる。

question: When did the footpath ban for PMDs come into effect in Singapore?
explanation: The footpath ban for PMDs came into effect on November 5, 2019, as part of the zero-tolerance approach due to a surge in accidents.
jpComparison: 日本でも2023年7月に電動キックボードの歩道走行が一部条件付きで解禁されたが、シンガポールは逆に2019年に歩道走行を全面禁止した。

question: Under the Active Mobility Act 2017, is it mandatory for individual riders to purchase Personal Mobility Insurance?
choices: A) Yes, it is mandatory for all individual riders
B) No, there is no mandatory requirement for individual riders yet
C) Yes, but only for riders under age 18
D) Yes, but only for e-scooter riders
explanation: There has not been any mandatory requirement for individual active mobility riders to purchase Personal Mobility Insurance yet. However, businesses must ensure their rider employees are covered.
jpComparison: 日本では2023年の法改正で電動キックボードは原付と同様の自賠責保険加入が必要。シンガポールでは個人のPMD保険義務化はまだ実現していない。

question: Under the Active Mobility Act 2017, which entities are required to ensure their riders are insured?
choices: B) Businesses that employ riders using active mobility devices on public paths
D) All persons above age 16 using PMDs
explanation: Businesses that employ riders using active mobility devices on public paths must ensure those individuals are insured against third-party liabilities.
jpComparison: 日本の業務使用車両と同様に、シンガポールでも事業者は業務中のライダーの第三者賠償保険を確保する義務がある。

question: In the sample Personal Liability Insurance policy, what is the annual premium for Plan A with S$1,000,000 limit of liability?
explanation: Plan A offers a Limit of Liability of S$1,000,000 with an annual premium of S$121.00. Plan B offers S$500,000 at S$85.00.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険は年間保険料1,000～3,000円程度で1億～無制限の補償が一般的。シンガポールのS$121（約1万円）は日本より割高。

question: What is the annual premium for Plan B Personal Liability Insurance with S$500,000 limit?
explanation: Plan B offers a Limit of Liability of S$500,000 with an annual premium of S$85.00.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険は火災保険や自動車保険の特約として数百円で付帯できることが多い。シンガポールでは単独ポリシーとしてS$85の設定。

question: Which of the following is NOT information typically required by insurers for underwriting Personal Liability Insurance?
choices: A) Name and address of the proposer
B) Number of children and pets
C) Detailed medical history of the proposer
explanation: Underwriting information is minimal: name, address, personal details (DOB, occupation), number of children/pets, and previous claims experience. Detailed medical history is not required.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険の引受でも健康告知は不要で、基本的な個人情報のみで加入できる点は同様。

question: How are premiums for Personal Liability Insurance generally determined?
choices: A) Based on the insured age and health status
B) Based on the insured occupation and income
C) Mostly standard premiums that vary with the level of sum insured chosen
D) Based on the property value and location
explanation: Premiums are mostly standard and vary only with the level of sum insured/limit of liability chosen.
jpComparison: 日本の個人賠償責任保険も保険金額（補償限度額）に応じた定額保険料が一般的で、個人のリスク評価はほぼ行われない点が類似。

question: The insurer may settle a liability claim on behalf of the insured. What happens if the insured refuses the recommended settlement?
choices: A) The insurer must continue to defend the case indefinitely
B) The insurer liability is capped at the amount the claim could have been settled for
C) The policy is automatically cancelled
D) The insured must pay double the settlement amount
explanation: If the insured refuses a settlement recommended by the insurer and elects to contest the claim, the insurer liability is limited to the amount for which the claim could have been settled, including legal costs up to the date of refusal.
jpComparison: 日本の賠償責任保険にも同様の条項があり、保険会社の和解提案を被保険者が拒否した場合、以降の費用は被保険者の自己負担となる。

question: What does the Claims Series Clause in a Personal Liability Insurance policy provide?
choices: A) Each individual claim is treated separately regardless of cause
B) All losses arising from the same cause are deemed one occurrence in the year the first loss occurred
C) Claims must be submitted in chronological order
D) The insurer will pay claims in a first-come-first-served basis
explanation: The Claims Series Clause provides that all losses arising from the same cause shall be deemed as one occurrence, having occurred during the policy year in which the first loss occurred.
jpComparison: 日本の賠償責任保険にも同一原因に基づく一連の損害を一事故とみなす条項がある。シンガポールでは最初の損害発生年度に帰属させる点が特徴。

question: A fire starts in an individual home and spreads to the neighbour property, causing damage. Is this covered under Personal Liability Insurance?
choices: A) No, fire damage is always excluded
B) Yes, this is a third-party property damage arising from the insured premises
C) No, this requires a separate Fire Insurance policy
D) Yes, but only if the insured deliberately started the fire
explanation: A fire spreading from the insured home to a neighbour property constitutes third-party property damage. The insured legal liability for this accidental damage is covered under Personal Liability Insurance.
jpComparison: 日本でも失火責任法により重過失がない限り失火者は賠償責任を負わないが、シンガポールでは過失による延焼も個人賠償責任保険の対象となる。

question: What is the difference between a licensee and an invitee in terms of a property owner duty of care?
choices: A) A licensee receives the highest duty of care
B) An invitee is on the premises for potential benefit to the owner and receives greater care than a licensee
C) There is no difference; both receive the same duty of care
D) A licensee is a trespasser with implied permission
explanation: An invitee (e.g. a customer) is on the premises with potential benefit to the owner and is owed the greatest care. A licensee (e.g. a social guest) is on the premises with consent but for the visitor own benefit and is owed less care.
jpComparison: 日本の民法では来訪者の法的地位による注意義務の段階区分は明確でないが、シンガポールのCommon Lawではinvitee/licensee/trespasserの3段階がある。

question: Which of the following statements about liability insurance coverage is CORRECT?
choices: A) Liability insurance covers a third party liability towards others
B) Liability insurance covers only the insured legal responsibility towards the third party
C) Liability insurance covers the insured liability to both third parties and family members
D) Liability insurance covers any loss regardless of legal liability
explanation: Liability insurance covers only the insured legal responsibility towards the third party. It does not cover a third party liability towards others.
jpComparison: 日本の賠償責任保険も被保険者の第三者に対する法律上の賠償責任のみを補償する点は同じ。第三者の他者への責任は対象外。

question: The insured must exercise reasonable precautions as a policy condition. Which legal concept does this relate to?
explanation: The policy condition requiring reasonable precautions relates to the legal concept of duty of care - the insured must act responsibly to prevent foreseeable harm.
jpComparison: 日本の損害保険でも被保険者の損害防止義務（善管注意義務）は一般的な保険条件。duty of careの概念と密接に関連。

question: Under a Personal Liability Insurance policy, the insurer has the right to take over and conduct the defence of any claim. What is this right known as?
choices: C) Conduct and control of proceedings
explanation: The insurer has the right to take over and conduct, in the name of the insured, the defence or settlement of any claim, and shall have full discretion in the conduct of proceedings and settlement.
jpComparison: 日本の賠償責任保険にも保険会社の示談交渉権（防御義務）がある。シンガポールではconduct and control of proceedingsとして保険約款に明記される。
